rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in type-level contracts.
Equation and constant declarations belong in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Assignments are allowed in implementation annexes when binding subcomponent outputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contracts.
Component type contracts must reference only visible features at that scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings like `assign` in component type annexes unless they refer to valid outputs.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports directly in type-level contracts.
